filtered.text: commerce.price Generates a price between min and max (inclusive). domain

search.detail:
commerce.price

Generates a price between min and max (inclusive).

Schema: awd.domain.commerce.price()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
dec	The number of decimal places. Example: 2
max	The maximum price. Example: 100
min	The minimum price. Example: 1
symbol	The currency value to use. Example: $
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
dec	integer	optional
max	number	optional
min	number	optional
symbol	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows commerce.price in use.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Full call: commerce.price(dec=2, max=10, min=1, symbol="$")

Returns: $4.79

Shows commerce.price when optional params are omitted.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: commerce.price()

Returns: 417.69

Shows commerce.price using dec.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (dec=2)

Full call: commerce.price(dec=2)

Returns: 417.69

Shows commerce.price using max.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (max=100)

Full call: commerce.price(max=100)

Returns: 42.29

Shows commerce.price using min.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (max=10, min=1)

Full call: commerce.price(max=10, min=1)

Returns: 4.79

Shows commerce.price using symbol.

Command: commerce.price

Params field: (symbol="$")

Full call: commerce.price(symbol="$")

Returns: $417.69

Open documentation

filtered.text: internet.email Generates data using faker internet email. domain

search.detail:
internet.email

Generates data using faker internet email.

Schema: awd.domain.internet.email()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
allowSpecialCharacters	Whether special characters such as .!#$%&'*+-/=?^_`{|}~ should be included in the email address.
firstName	The optional first name to use.
lastName	The optional last name to use.
provider	The mail provider domain to use. If not specified, a random free mail provider will be chosen.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
allowSpecialCharacters	boolean	optional
firstName	string	optional
lastName	string	optional
provider	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows internet.email when optional params are omitted.

Command: internet.email

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: internet.email()

Returns: Edwin.Dibbert@hotmail.com

Shows internet.email using allowSpecialCharacters.

Command: internet.email

Params field: (allowSpecialCharacters=true)

Full call: internet.email(allowSpecialCharacters=true)

Returns: Edwin.Dibbert@hotmail.com

Shows internet.email using firstName.

Command: internet.email

Params field: (firstName="Ada")

Full call: internet.email(firstName="Ada")

Returns: Ada.Gutmann9@hotmail.com

Shows internet.email using lastName.

Command: internet.email

Params field: (lastName="Lovelace")

Full call: internet.email(lastName="Lovelace")

Returns: Edwin.Lovelace9@hotmail.com

Shows internet.email using provider.

Command: internet.email

Params field: (provider="example.com")

Full call: internet.email(provider="example.com")

Returns: Aaliyah.Bosco@example.com

Open documentation

filtered.text: number.int Returns a single random integer between zero and the given max value or the given range. domain

search.detail:
number.int

Returns a single random integer between zero and the given max value or the given range.

Schema: awd.domain.number.int()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
min	Optional minimum integer.
max	Optional maximum integer.
multipleOf	Generated number will be a multiple of the given integer.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
min	number	optional
max	number	optional
multipleOf	number	optional
Usage Examples

Shows number.int when optional params are omitted.

Command: number.int

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: number.int()

Returns: 3756200289967619

Shows number.int using min.

Command: number.int

Params field: (max=10, min=1)

Full call: number.int(max=10, min=1)

Returns: 5

Shows number.int using max.

Command: number.int

Params field: (max=5)

Full call: number.int(max=5)

Returns: 2

Shows number.int using multipleOf.

Command: number.int

Params field: (multipleOf=1)

Full call: number.int(multipleOf=1)

Returns: 3756200289967619

Open documentation

filtered.text: string.uuid Returns a UUID (Universally Unique Identifier). domain

search.detail:
string.uuid

Returns a UUID (Universally Unique Identifier).

Schema: awd.domain.string.uuid()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
version	The specific UUID version to use. If refDate is supplied and version is omitted, version 7 is used automatically.
refDate	The timestamp to encode into the UUID. This is only valid for UUID v7. If refDate is supplied and version is omitted, version 7 is used automatically. Providing refDate with version 4 is invalid.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
version	4|7	optional
refDate	string|number|date	optional
Usage Examples

Shows string.uuid when optional params are omitted.

Command: string.uuid

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: string.uuid()

Returns: 6b042125-686a-43e0-8a68-23cf5bee102e

Shows string.uuid using version.

Command: string.uuid

Params field: (version=7)

Full call: string.uuid(version=7)

Returns: 019edb71-aa40-76b0-8421-25686a3e0a68

Shows string.uuid using refDate.

Command: string.uuid

Params field: (refDate="2026-06-18T00:00:00.000Z")

Full call: string.uuid(refDate="2026-06-18T00:00:00.000Z")

Returns: 019ed807-0800-76b0-8421-25686a3e0a68

Open documentation

search.detail:
helpers.arrayElement

Returns one random element from the supplied array.

Schema: faker.helpers.arrayElement()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
array	Array of candidate values to choose from.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
array	array	required
Usage Examples

Shows helpers.arrayElement in use.

Command: helpers.arrayElement

Params field: (["A", "B", "C"])

Full call: helpers.arrayElement(["A", "B", "C"])

Returns: B
